segments.text: Now it came to pass, as David sat in his house, that David said to Nathan the prophet,
Lo, I dwell in an house of cedars, but the ark of the covenant of the LORD remaineth under curtains.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Then Nathan said unto David,
Do all that is in thine heart; for God is with thee.
segments.speaker: Nathan

segments.text: And it came to pass the same night, that the word of God came to Nathan, saying,
Go and tell David my servant, Thou shalt not build me an house to dwell in:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I have not dwelt in an house since the day that I brought up Israel unto this day; but have gone from tent to tent, and from one tabernacle to another.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Wheresoever I have walked with all Israel, spake I a word to any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to feed my people, saying, Why have ye not built me an house of cedars?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Now therefore thus shalt thou say unto my servant David, I took thee from the sheepcote, even from following the sheep, that thou shouldest be ruler over my people Israel:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And I have been with thee whithersoever thou hast walked, and have cut off all thine enemies from before thee, and have made thee a name like the name of the great men that are in the earth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Also I will ordain a place for my people Israel, and will plant them, and they shall dwell in their place, and shall be moved no more; neither shall the children of wickedness waste them any more, as at the beginning,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And since the time that I commanded judges to be over my people Israel. Moreover I will subdue all thine enemies. Furthermore I tell thee that the LORD will build thee an house.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And it shall come to pass, when thy days be expired that thou must go to be with thy fathers, that I will raise up thy seed after thee, which shall be of thy sons; and I will establish his kingdom.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He shall build me an house, and I will stablish his throne for ever.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will be his father, and he shall be my son: and I will not take my mercy away from him, as I took it from him that was before thee:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But I will settle him in mine house and in my kingdom for ever: and his throne shall be established for evermore.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: According to all these words, and according to all this vision, so did Nathan speak unto David.
And David the king came and sat before the LORD, and said,
Who am I, O LORD God, and what is mine house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And yet this was a small thing in thine eyes, O God; for thou hast also spoken of thy servant's house for a great while to come, and hast regarded me according to the estate of a man of high degree, O LORD God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: What can David speak more to thee for the honour of thy servant? for thou knowest thy servant.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O LORD, for thy servant's sake, and according to thine own heart, hast thou done all this greatness, in making known all these great things.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O LORD, there is none like thee, neither is there any God beside thee, according to all that we have heard with our ears.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: And what one nation in the earth is like thy people Israel, whom God went to redeem to be his own people, to make thee a name of greatness and terribleness, by driving out nations from before thy people, whom thou hast redeemed out of Egypt?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For thy people Israel didst thou make thine own people for ever; and thou, LORD, becamest their God.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Therefore now, LORD, let the thing that thou hast spoken concerning thy servant and concerning his house be established for ever, and do as thou hast said.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Let it even be established, that thy name may be magnified for ever, saying, The LORD of hosts is the God of Israel, even a God to Israel: and let the house of David thy servant be established before thee.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: For thou, O my God, hast told thy servant that thou wilt build him an house: therefore thy servant hath found in his heart to pray before thee.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: hast…: Heb. hast revealed the ear of thy servant

segments.text: And now, LORD, thou art God, and hast promised this goodness unto thy servant:
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Now therefore let it please thee to bless the house of thy servant, that it may be before thee for ever: for thou blessest, O LORD, and it shall be blessed for ever.
segments.speaker: David

footnotes: let…: or, it hath pleased thee
